title: Cathay Shares Jump as Carrier Flags Strong First-Half Profit
summary: Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd. shares jumped as much as 3% after the carrier signaled a rise in first-half net income, driven by strong passenger and cargo demand despite the Iran war-induced oil-price shock.

summary: Spanish banks are taking a new step to chip away at the dominance of US payment systems such as Visa Inc. and Mastercard Inc. by offering a new bank-to-bank payment system in stores.

title: Gold Extends Gains as Dip-Buyers Shrug Off US-Iran Escalation
summary: Gold extended gains, supported by dip-buyers, despite escalating Middle East tensions.

title: Yen Rebounds From Four-Decade Low as BOJ May Hike Rates Faster
summary: The yen weakened past 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

title: UK Stocks an Underappreciated AI Play, Brooks Macdonald CIO Says
summary: The UK is an underappreciated knowledge-sector superpower and remains a “happy zone” for active stock-picking, according to William Hobbs, chief investment officer at Brooks Macdonald Asset Management.

title: Alphabet Earnings Need to Show Cloud Growth as AI Spending Soars
summary: With AI spending skyrocketing, investors are looking for Alphabet Inc.’s earnings to show strong growth in its cloud-computing business, which would demonstrate a clear return on the company’s investments.

title: Stocks Fall as Caution Builds Ahead of Alphabet: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell ahead of Alphabet Inc.’s earnings as traders waited for the clearest read yet on whether the vast sums technology hyperscalers are pouring into artificial intelligence will pay off.

title: Funds’ Bullish Wagers on European Gas Jump the Most Since War
summary: Investment funds and other speculators boosted their net-long positions in European gas by the most since the first week of the Iran war, signaling a strong conviction that prices still have room to rise as the conflict darkens the supply outlook ahead of winter.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 22
summary: Oil extended gains as the US and Iran played down the prospect of talks and disruptions to global supplies continued to mount.

title: PHP/USD: Philippines Joins India in Stepping Currency Defense as Oil Rises
summary: The Philippine central bank intervened in the foreign-exchange market as the peso fell to a record low, joining India in protecting currencies hit by surging oil prices.

title: TPG Eyes Tokyo Sale to Japan Hotel REIT for $860 Million
summary: Investment fund TPG Angelo Gordon Inc. plans to sell a Tokyo hotel property to Japan Hotel REIT Investment Co. as a tourism boom unfolds and boosts valuations, people familiar with the matter said.

title: MSEDCL Said to Pick Banks for India IPO Worth Up to $1 Billion
summary: Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Co., the power distribution company in India’s richest state, has shortlisted six investment banks for a planned initial public offering that could raise between $500 million and $1 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

title: JPY/USD: Why Is Japan Yen So Weak? Can Government Intervention Help Currency?
summary: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its role in driving up import prices and household living costs.

title: Key Oil Chokepoints in Mideast Face Disruption as Threats Mount
summary: Shipping disruptions in key Middle East chokepoints intensified as the US-Iran war grinds on, with vessels in the Red Sea making unusual maneuvers, while visible traffic through the Strait of Hormuz sank.

title: South African Deputy Finance Chief Faces Potential PIC Ouster
summary: South Africa’s deputy finance minister said he’ll submit a formal response defending his position as chairman of the Public Investment Corp. when its board meets next week to discuss recent turmoil at the state pension-fund manager.

title: Thai Bonds Are Beating Asia and Analysts Predict More Gains
summary: Thailand’s bonds have beaten their emerging Asian peers over the past month and analysts predict they will extend gains on favorable supply dynamics and slower inflation.

title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Inflation Slows More Than Expected in June
Rare Earths Giant Lynas Shares Fall to Five-Month Low After Revenue Miss
summary: Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. shares dropped to a five-month low after Australia’s largest rare-earths producer reported quarterly sales that missed analysts’ expectations.

title: Indian Refiners Halt Iraq Oil Loadings on Rising Hormuz Risk
summary: Two Indian state-run refiners have suspended crude oil loadings from Iraq as mounting security risks in the Strait of Hormuz make it increasingly difficult to ensure safety of vessels, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Copper Wavers Near $14,000 as Traders Watch for Trump’s Tariff
summary: Copper pulled back from its highest level since early June as traders watched for any announcement from the White House on its plans for import tariffs on the metal.

title: Japan 40-Year Bond Sale Sees Strongest Demand Since March 2025
summary: Japan’s 40-year government bond auction drew its strongest demand since March 2025 as investors were attracted to higher yields.

title: Wheat Gains On Black Sea Risks and Corn Hits Two-Month High
summary: Wheat rose for a second session on escalating Black Sea tensions and worsening crop prospects.
